title: The stray dog behind the shop
summary: Narrate slowly feeding and protecting a frightened stray dog behind a shop until it learns to trust people again.
topic: Caring for a stray dog over time
prompt: Write a short story (8–10 sentences) about an animal you helped little by little over many days. Describe how it looked at first, what you did each day, and how it slowly changed. Use past simple for the story, present simple for the habit now, and 'because' to explain your choices.
hintStarters: Behind the small shop where I work, there was a thin brown dog that…
Every morning I left a little food because…
After two weeks, the dog finally…
sampleText: Behind the small shop where I work, there was a thin brown dog. At first it was very afraid, and it hid in a dark corner. It ran away every time a person walked near. The dog was hungry, but it did not trust anyone. Every morning I left a little food in a bowl by the wall because I did not want the dog to starve. For many days, I sat far away and did not look at it. The dog ate the food only after I went back inside. Slowly, day by day, it came a little closer to me. After two weeks, the dog finally took the food from my hand. Its tail moved a little, and then it wagged hard. Now the dog waits for me every morning, and it is not afraid anymore. I gave it a name, and I call it Coco. People say a stray dog is not worth the trouble, but Coco trusts me now. A little food and a lot of patience changed one frightened life.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một con chó gầy màu nâu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: it hid in a dark corner
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nó nấp trong một góc tối

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I left a little food in a bowl
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi để lại một ít thức ăn trong một cái bát

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I did not want the dog to starve
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi không muốn con chó chết đói

sampleAnnotations.phrase: it came a little closer to me
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nó tiến lại gần tôi một chút

sampleAnnotations.phrase: took the food from my hand
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lấy thức ăn từ tay tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: A little food and a lot of patience changed one frightened life
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một ít thức ăn và rất nhiều kiên nhẫn đã thay đổi một sinh mạng từng sợ hãi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for the story, present simple for now
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để kể chuyện ('it was', 'it hid', 'I left', 'it took') và hiện tại đơn cho thói quen hiện tại ('the dog waits', 'it is not afraid anymore'). Lưu ý động từ bất quy tắc: 'hide → hid', 'take → took'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past simple to tell the story ('it was', 'it hid', 'I left', 'it took') and the present simple for the habit now ('the dog waits', 'it is not afraid anymore'). Watch irregular verbs: 'hide → hid', 'take → took'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'because' to explain a choice
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để giải thích lý do bạn làm điều gì: 'I left a little food because I did not want the dog to starve.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' to explain why you did something: 'I left a little food in a bowl because I did not want the dog to starve.'

sampleTranslationVi: Phía sau cửa hàng nhỏ nơi tôi làm việc, có một con chó gầy màu nâu. Lúc đầu nó rất sợ hãi, và nó nấp trong một góc tối. Nó chạy đi mỗi khi có người đến gần. Con chó đói, nhưng nó không tin ai cả. Mỗi sáng tôi để lại một ít thức ăn trong một cái bát cạnh tường vì tôi không muốn con chó chết đói. Trong nhiều ngày, tôi ngồi xa ra và không nhìn nó. Con chó chỉ ăn thức ăn sau khi tôi vào trong. Từ từ, ngày qua ngày, nó tiến lại gần tôi một chút. Sau hai tuần, con chó cuối cùng đã lấy thức ăn từ tay tôi. Cái đuôi của nó động đậy một chút, rồi nó vẫy mạnh. Bây giờ con chó đợi tôi mỗi sáng, và nó không còn sợ nữa. Tôi đặt cho nó một cái tên, và tôi gọi nó là Coco. Người ta nói một con chó hoang không đáng để bận tâm, nhưng Coco tin tôi bây giờ. Một ít thức ăn và rất nhiều kiên nhẫn đã thay đổi một sinh mạng từng sợ hãi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence opens the story in the past most naturally?
options: Behind the small shop where I work, there were a thin brown dog.
Behind the small shop where I work, there was a thin brown dog it.
Behind the small shop where I work, a thin brown dog there was one.
Behind the small shop where I work, there was a thin brown dog.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'there was' is the singular past of 'there is', correct for 'a dog'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the frightened dog in the past?
options: At first it was very afraid, and it hid in a dark corner.
At first it was very afraid, and it hide in a dark corner.
At first it is very afraid, and it hid in a dark corner.
At first it was very afraid, and in a dark corner it hided.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'was' and 'hid' (irregular past of 'hide') are correct past forms.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence contrasts hunger and fear correctly?
options: The dog was hungry, but it did not trusted anyone.
The dog was hungry, but it did not trust anyone.
The dog were hungry, but it did not trust anyone.
The dog was hungry, but anyone it did not trust it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'was' is singular past; after 'did not' use the base verb 'trust'; 'but' shows contrast.
mcQuestions.hint: Use the base verb after 'did not'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why you left food, with 'because'?
options: Every morning I left a little food in a bowl because of I did not want the dog to starve.
Every morning I leave a little food in a bowl because I did not wanted the dog to starve.
Every morning I left a little food in a bowl because I did not want the dog to starve.
Every morning I left a little food in a bowl because the dog to starve I did not want it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'left' and 'did not want' are correct past forms; 'because' is followed by a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the past verbs and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows the slow change over time?
options: Slow, day by day, it came a little closer to me.
Slowly, day by day, it come a little closer to me.
Slowly, day by day, a little closer to me it came it.
Slowly, day by day, it came a little closer to me.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Slowly' is the adverb and 'came' is the correct past of 'come'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the adverb form and the past of 'come'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence reports the turning point in the past?
options: After two weeks, the dog finally took the food from my hand.
After two weeks, the dog finally take the food from my hand.
After two weeks, the dog finally taked the food from my hand.
After two weeks, the food from my hand the dog finally took it.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the tail moving most naturally?
options: It's tail moved a little, and then it wagged hard.
Its tail moved a little, and then it wagged hard.
Its tail move a little, and then it wag hard.
Its tail moved a little, and then hard it wagging.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Its' is the correct possessive; 'moved' and 'wagged' are past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Choose the possessive 'Its' and keep the past tense.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the dog now, in the present?
options: Now the dog wait for me every morning, and it is not afraid anymore.
Now the dog waits for me every morning, and it are not afraid anymore.
Now the dog waits for me every morning, and it is not afraid anymore.
Now the dog waits for me every morning, and not afraid anymore it is it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'waits' agrees with 'the dog', and 'it is' is the correct present form of 'be'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb ending and the form of 'be'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence says what you do now most naturally?
options: I gave it a name, and I calls it Coco.
I give it a name, and I call it Coco yesterday.
I gave it a name, and Coco I call it it.
I gave it a name, and I call it Coco.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'gave' is the past for the naming, and 'I call' (present, no -s) is the current habit.
mcQuestions.hint: One verb is a past action, the other is a present habit with 'I'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states the main idea most naturally?
options: A little food and a lot of patience changed one frightened life.
A little food and a lot of patience change one frightened life.
A little food and a lot of patience changed one frightened lives.
A little food and a lot of patience changed one frightened life completely globally forever.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'changed' is the correct past simple, and 'life' is singular here.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep the past tense and the singular noun.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some people say a stray dog is not worth the trouble; how do you decide whether one small life is worth your time and care?
